response.justification: The candidate is an experienced, versatile senior engineer, but the profile does not match this role's core requirements. His C++ exposure is limited to a seven-month stint at National Instruments in 2014–2015 and a university thesis project, far short of the mandatory 5–10 years of hands-on Windows C++ development. There is no evidence of SQL Server or Oracle depth, and no Fixed Income, Capital Markets, or Investment Banking domain experience — RIDE Capital was a German retail investment startup on a Node/TypeScript stack, not a capital-markets environment. The strengths he brings (TypeScript, Node.js, DLang, testing culture) sit outside what this consulting engagement demands.
strengths: Long, varied engineering career with strong software craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD, pairing)
Proven low-latency systems experience (high-throughput D-language services at Dunnhumby)
Excellent communication skills evidenced by workshops, talks, and courses
concerns: Only ~7 months of professional C++ experience, and none on Windows-focused financial systems
No Fixed Income, derivatives, or capital-markets/investment-banking domain background
No demonstrated SQL Server or Oracle experience; database work centers on MongoDB/MySQL

key_factors.factor: Strong general engineering and communication skills
